action.reasoning: Observation: The navmesh approach fails because pf.snap_point(human_pos) returns NaN - the GPS-derived human position is outside the navmesh. The robot gets from 9m to 4.28m in 200 steps with pure GPS control but oscillates. The GPS angle oscillates ±π meaning human is behind. Analysis: Need pure GPS-compass control without navmesh. The key fix: when human is behind (|angle| > π/2), spin in place aggressively. When human is in front, move forward fast. Add proper stuck recovery. The navmesh approach is fundamentally broken because human positions are off-navmesh. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with pure GPS-compass control. Use high angular velocity when turning, high linear velocity when aligned. Add stuck recovery that spins 180°. Submit eval immediately.
